raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes revisiting the framing of selective prediction, calibration, and the contribution claim above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The draft's argument depends on whether ECE, confidence, abstention, and deployment recommendations follow coherently from one another.","R1.3":"The request entails checking whether the reported ECE results actually support the claims about selective-prediction ability and model choice.","R1.5":"A draft-level improvement can reasonably involve reorganizing the introduction, methods framing, results interpretation, and practitioner recommendation arc.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation is a broad request to improve a research draft rather than answer a narrow technical question.","R1.7":"The draft invokes prior work and canonical-metric claims, so responsible improvement includes checking attribution, citation accuracy, and literature coverage.","RX.1":"Any projection of requested improvement must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the artifact alone.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong deployment and sufficiency claims, making overclaim avoidance an always-active responsibility.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than conducting new experiments or executing the research agenda.","RX.4":"Because the draft discusses safety-critical deployment, avoiding downstream harm from unsupported recommendations is always active.","RX.5":"Responsible improvement requires traceability between proposed changes and the artifact's claims, metrics, citations, and reported results."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes revisiting the framing of selective prediction, calibration, and the contribution claim above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The draft's argument depends on whether ECE, confidence, abstention, and deployment recommendations follow coherently from one another.
rationale.R1.3: The request entails checking whether the reported ECE results actually support the claims about selective-prediction ability and model choice.
rationale.R1.5: A draft-level improvement can reasonably involve reorganizing the introduction, methods framing, results interpretation, and practitioner recommendation arc.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation is a broad request to improve a research draft rather than answer a narrow technical question.
rationale.R1.7: The draft invokes prior work and canonical-metric claims, so responsible improvement includes checking attribution, citation accuracy, and literature coverage.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of requested improvement must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong deployment and sufficiency claims, making overclaim avoidance an always-active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than conducting new experiments or executing the research agenda.
rationale.RX.4: Because the draft discusses safety-critical deployment, avoiding downstream harm from unsupported recommendations is always active.
rationale.RX.5: Responsible improvement requires traceability between proposed changes and the artifact's claims, metrics, citations, and reported results.
